plugin_desc: Inspur AS5500 storage-specific SNMP collection for Inspur AS5500 enterprise storage arrays (Inspur private MIB, IANA PEN 48512). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects the Inspur private OIDs for device health — the managed-disk status (statuaDisk, one row per managed disk), the physical drive availability (driveStatus, one row per drive) and the power-supply status (powerStatus, one row per power supply). The disk and drive raw codes are folded into the shared disk-health metric and the power-supply codes into the power metric, normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault, with the worst component taken per device with max(). The vendor enum value codes have not been verified on a live device, so conservatively only 1 is treated as healthy and any other code as a fault. This profile does not publish a pollable controller CPU/memory utilization gauge, chassis temperature, or fan status, so CPU, memory, temperature and fan are not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — Inspur AS5500 series enterprise storage arrays with SNMP enabled.
name: Storage

metrics.name: device_disk_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the physical disk health of the Inspur AS5500 storage device, derived from the managed-disk status (statuaDisk) and physical drive availability (driveStatus) tables. The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other code = fault (the vendor enum value codes are not yet field-verified), and the worst disk is taken per device with max(). A value greater than 1 indicates a degraded or failed disk that carries a risk of data loss and should be replaced immediately.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the Inspur AS5500 storage device (powerStatus, one row per power supply). The raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and any other code = fault (the vendor enum value codes are not yet field-verified), and the worst power supply is taken per device with max(). A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply fault or loss of power redundancy that warrants investigation.
